title: Fix doc-prose drift found in beta.85 story audit
description: Post-beta.85 audit verified all 11 stories run clean / tests pass (no source changes needed), but found doc-prose drift where the markdown describes behavior that no longer matches beta.85. Code is correct in every case; only docs/*.md prose is wrong. Fix the factual-drift items; the additive new-feature demos and version-label nits are optional.
design:
FACTUAL DRIFT (docs wrong vs verified beta.85 behavior) — fix these:
1. [HIGH] docs/07-http-client.md:94-100 — 'Error renames from v3' table lists '"StatusCodeError"' / '"TransportError"' as v4 tags to catch. VERIFIED WRONG: beta.85's only channel _tag is 'HttpClientError' (HttpClientError.d.ts:13); granularity is via error.reason, whose union is RequestError | ResponseError (HttpClientError.d.ts:232) — the names StatusCodeError/TransportError do not exist. A reader following the table would write catchTag('StatusCodeError') which never fires. Story code (stories/07:82,102) correctly catches 'HttpClientError'. FIX: rewrite table to: catch 'HttpClientError', then branch on error.reason._tag (RequestError/ResponseError).
2. [MED] docs/07-http-client.md:111 — claims HttpClientResponse.schemaBodyJson 'was removed'. VERIFIED still exported (HttpClientResponse.d.ts:29). FIX: keep the manual-decode guidance but drop the false 'was removed' justification (reason: generic factory schema can't satisfy its constraints).
3. [HIGH] docs/04-tagged-errors.md:78 — says Schema.Defect() 'turns the inner Error/object into something round-trippable.' VERIFIED at runtime: an Error round-trips to an Error, but a plain object {message,extra} decodes back to an Error (extra field lost, JSON.stringify(decoded)==={}). Per b76 Defect models unknown w/ JSON encoding; only Error-shaped JSON decodes to Error. FIX: drop/qualify the '/object' claim — arbitrary objects do not survive unchanged.
4. [MED] docs/03-schema-validation-and-decode.md:92-100 — SchemaIssue rename table omits 'InvalidValue' (SchemaIssue.d.ts:517), which is what a failing Schema.check constraint actually produces and why the story prints 'Invalid data "bad"' (not the custom 'Invalid email format'). FIX: add an InvalidValue row (value is correct type but violates a constraint).

OPTIONAL DOC NOTES (behavior correct, prose could add a caveat):
5. [MED] docs/06-config-and-providers.md (~47-73) — note b81: Config.withDefault now recovers only from MISSING data; a present-but-invalid value (e.g. REQUEST_TIMEOUT='abc') now propagates a validation error instead of falling back. Story behavior is correct; add a one-line caveat.
6. [LOW] docs/06 — b75 Redacted is encodable-by-default now; current prose ('hides from casual stringification') stays accurate, only add if clarifying serialization.
7. [LOW] docs/01-basics.md:111 + docs/03:70 — '(v4-beta.57)' version labels still factually true at beta.85; optionally relabel to avoid implying staleness.

ADDITIVE NEW-FEATURE DEMOS (no existing code is wrong; bigger scope, separate work if desired):
- Effect.transposeOption (b84), Random.choice (b85), Schedule.tap (b71) — chapter 10 candidates.
- Effect.try direct-thunk form (b84) — chapter 01 additive example.
- @effect/vitest memo-fork for nested it.layer (b67), TestClock no-ambient-Scope fix (b70) — chapter 11 informational notes.
- HttpClient.withRateLimiter / responseMode:'response-only' (b85 surface) — chapter 07 unexercised APIs.
acceptance_criteria: docs/07 error table rewritten (catch HttpClientError, branch on reason._tag); docs/07 schemaBodyJson 'removed' claim corrected; docs/04:78 Defect round-trip prose qualified; docs/03 InvalidValue row added. Optional notes (5-7) applied or explicitly deferred. typecheck/vitest/bun:test stay green (doc-only changes). Additive feature demos tracked separately if pursued.
close_reason: Audited all 11 stories under beta.85 — every story runs clean / tests pass; no broken code. Fixed verified doc-prose drift: (1) docs/07 error table rewritten — only channel _tag is 'HttpClientError'; specific failures are on error.reason (RequestError=Transport/Encode/InvalidUrl, ResponseError=StatusCode/Decode/EmptyBody); (2) docs/07 'schemaBodyJson removed' was false, corrected; also caught+fixed a fabricated 'responseMode: response-only' claim (real API is retryTransient({retryOn})), verified against source; (3) docs/04 Schema.Defect() round-trip prose qualified (objects normalize to bare Error, runtime-verified); (4) docs/03 added InvalidValue note; (5) docs/06 b81 withDefault missing-only caveat; (6) docs/01+03 stale beta.57 labels refreshed. ADOPTED new features with runnable demos + docs: story 10 Schedule.tap (§5d), Effect.transposeOption (§9), Random.choice (§10); story 01 Effect.try bare-thunk (§5b). Gates green: typecheck 0 errors, vitest 6/6, bun:test 1/1, all touched stories run. EFFECT_CHANGELOG candidate notes marked demonstrated.

title: Bump effect deps beta.66 → beta.85 + apply audit findings
description: Latest published effect beta is 4.0.0-beta.85; package.json pins effect and @effect/vitest at ^4.0.0-beta.66 (~19 betas behind). Bump both pins to ^4.0.0-beta.85, run bun install, then run gates: bun run typecheck, bun run test (vitest), bun run test:bun. Apply the storybook/doc audit findings below (scanned from the beta.67-85 changelog), then extend docs/EFFECT_CHANGELOG.md (currently documented through beta.66) with the relevant beta.67-85 entries and bump the target line. Skip findings that don't apply after inspection.
design:
AUDIT FINDINGS from beta.67-85 changelog (Effect-TS/effect-smol), breaking-first:

BREAKING — likely to break current story code:
1. [beta.76] Schema.Defect/Schema.Error changed from CONSTANTS to CONSTRUCTOR FUNCTIONS: Schema.Defect() / Schema.Error(). stories/04-tagged-errors.ts:36 and stories/09-jsonrpc-schema-factory.ts:67 use 'cause: Schema.Defect' as a bare constant — must become 'Schema.Defect()'. Also update docs/04 prose. Note: Defect now models unknown with JSON encoding; non-Error objects like {message:'boom'} no longer round-trip unchanged. ErrorWithStack -> Error({includeStack:true}), DefectWithStack -> Defect({includeStack:true}).
2. [beta.67] SchemaParser.makeUnsafe renamed to SchemaParser.make. (grep clean in stories, but verify after bump.)
3. [beta.68] Schema.asserts/SchemaParser.asserts now assert directly: asserts(schema, input); Schema.Codec.ToAsserts removed.
4. [beta.68] Random.nextUUIDv4 REMOVED — use new platform-agnostic Crypto service (randomUUIDv4/randomUUIDv7). (grep clean; verify.)
5. [beta.68] Model.Generated renamed to Model.GeneratedByDb.
6. [beta.75] Types.MergeRecord alias removed — use Types.MergeLeft.
7. [beta.84] Config.make low-level constructor no longer exported; ConfigProvider.orElse/nested/mapInput composition semantics changed; ConfigProvider.fromDir returns undefined when absent. Check stories/06 + docs/06 ConfigProvider usage.

BEHAVIORAL — verify documented behavior still holds:
8. [beta.81] Config.withDefault now only recovers from MISSING data for literal/union schemas; invalid present values now propagate validation errors; no longer recovers from filter failures. stories/06 uses Config.withDefault('localhost') and Config.withDefault(Duration.seconds(30)) — re-verify these still behave as documented.
9. [beta.84] SchemaError now extends Data.TaggedError (also a native Error); SchemaParser Promise APIs reject Error whose cause is the Issue; adapter error normalization across is/asserts/Promise/Sync/Result/Option/make. Re-verify chapter 11 typed-error / schema-decode assertions.
10. [beta.67] @effect/vitest now forks memo maps for nested it.layer suites (sibling setup isolated, parent sharing preserved); [beta.70] TestClock fix when layer provided without ambient Scope. Note in chapter 11 if relevant.

NEW FEATURES — consider adopting:
11. [beta.84] Effect.transposeOption (Option<Effect<A,E,R>> -> Effect<Option<A>,E,R>) — story 10 composing-effects.
12. [beta.84] Effect.try now accepts a thunk directly (matches tryPromise) — story 01/04 could simplify.
13. [beta.85] Random.choice; [beta.71] Schedule.tap; [beta.71] .value reintroduced on Schema.Array/NonEmptyArray; [beta.68] Stream.broadcastN.
14. [beta.81] HTTP API streaming response support; [beta.82/83] HttpApi inference + d.ts fixes — relevant to story 07 / HttpApiTest follow-up.
15. [beta.77] OTEL env-var configuration (OtlpResource) — relates to prior OTel logger work.

CHANGELOG: docs/EFFECT_CHANGELOG.md is documented through beta.66; add beta.67-85 entries (at least items 1,7,8,9,11,14,15) and bump the target line. The existing 'ConfigProvider.fromUnknown underscore split (if that lands)' note did NOT land in 67-85 — leave the nested-object workaround in story 06.
acceptance_criteria: package.json pins effect + @effect/vitest at ^4.0.0-beta.85; bun install clean; typecheck no errors; vitest green; bun:test green; Schema.Defect()/Schema.Error() constructor migration applied in stories/04 + stories/09 (+ docs); docs/EFFECT_CHANGELOG.md extended past beta.66 with relevant entries and target line bumped; non-applicable findings explicitly noted in close reason.
close_reason: Bumped effect + @effect/vitest ^4.0.0-beta.66 → ^4.0.0-beta.85; bun install clean. Gates green: typecheck 0 errors, vitest 6/6, bun:test 1/1, story 04 runs at runtime. BREAKING applied: b76 Schema.Defect/Error now constructor functions — migrated 'cause: Schema.Defect' → 'Schema.Defect()' in stories/04, stories/09, docs/04 (7 type errors fixed). Other removed/renamed APIs (SchemaParser.makeUnsafe→make, Random.nextUUIDv4 removed, Types.MergeRecord→MergeLeft, Model.Generated→GeneratedByDb, Config.make unexported, Schema.asserts/Codec.ToAsserts) confirmed UNUSED in storybook. Behavioral b81 (Config.withDefault missing-data-only) + b84 (SchemaError extends Data.TaggedError, ConfigProvider composition) re-verified — chapters 06/11 unaffected. docs/EFFECT_CHANGELOG.md extended with beta.67-85 highlights, target moved to beta.85, quick-map + watchlist refreshed. NOT adopted (documented as candidates, follow-up if desired): new features Effect.transposeOption (b84), Effect.try direct thunk (b84), Random.choice (b85), Schedule.tap (b71), Schema.Array.value (b71) — left as optional story enhancements. ConfigProvider.fromUnknown underscore-split did NOT land in 67-85, story 06 nested-object workaround stays.

title: Note SchemaTransformation decoding error message normalization
description: Improvement: SchemaTransformation now produces consistent decoding error messages. Add a changelog bullet. ALSO verify story snapshots in docs/03-schema-validation-and-decode.md and stories/03 still match — error-message strings are the most likely thing to drift.
close_reason: Added bullet to docs/EFFECT_CHANGELOG.md beta.60 covering urlFromString/dateTimeUtcFromString/dateTimeZonedFromString message changes. Story 03 does not snapshot these specific transformations, so no story update needed.

title: Adopt Config.literals in config-and-providers story
description: beta.60 added Config.literals as a convenience constructor for Schema.Literals-backed config values. Watchlist flags it. Verify changelog entry under beta.60 and demonstrate in stories/06-config-and-providers.ts for any literal-union config field (e.g. log level, environment name).
close_reason: Adopted Config.literals in stories/06-config-and-providers.ts and docs/06-config-and-providers.md. Replaced the Schema.Literals + Config.schema pair with Config.literals(ENV_VALUES, 'APP_ENV') for the literal-union case. Watchlist entry removed from EFFECT_CHANGELOG.md. Verified by running the story.

title: Adopt Schema.DurationFromString in schema/config stories
description: beta.60 added Schema.DurationFromString for parsing duration strings into Duration. Watchlist in docs/EFFECT_CHANGELOG.md flags it. Verify the changelog entry is present under beta.60, then replace any hand-rolled Duration transforms in stories/02-schema-data-modeling.ts or stories/06-config-and-providers.ts with the built-in.
close_reason: Added REQUEST_TIMEOUT field demoing Config.schema(Schema.DurationFromString, ...) in both stories/06 and docs/06. Watchlist entry retired. Verified by running the story; provider parses '5 seconds' and the testLayer uses Duration.seconds(1).

title: Document Effect.firstSuccessOf in changelog and composing-effects story
description: v4 re-introduces Effect.firstSuccessOf(iterable) — sequential fallback that returns the first successful effect. If all fail, fails with the LAST error; empty iterable defects with 'Received an empty collection of effects'. Distinct from Effect.race (parallel, first-to-complete) and orElse (binary chain). Use case: prioritized fallback (primary API → secondary → cache). Add changelog entry and an example in stories/10-composing-effects.ts.
close_reason: Added stories/10 section 5c (runnable example), docs/10 'Sequential fallback' section, and a takeaways bullet. Contrast vs race / orElse and the empty-iterable defect noted. Story output verified.

title: Document Effect.acquireDisposable and add to services-and-layers story
description: Effect.acquireDisposable (Effect.ts:6225) wraps Effect<A extends Disposable | AsyncDisposable> as a Scope-bound resource using Symbol.dispose / Symbol.asyncDispose. Lighter than acquireRelease when the resource implements TC39 Explicit Resource Management (e.g. node:sqlite DatabaseSync, anything constructed with using). Returns Effect<A, E, R | Scope>. Add changelog entry and a usage example in stories/05-services-and-layers.ts.
close_reason: Added story 05 section 10 with a Disposable Connection class and Effect.acquireDisposable inside a Scope; added 'Disposable resources' section in docs/05 contrasting with Effect.acquireRelease. Takeaways updated. Verified end-to-end with bun stories/05-services-and-layers.ts.

title: Document HttpApiTest module and add HttpApi story coverage
description: effect now ships packages/effect/src/unstable/httpapi/HttpApiTest.ts with HttpApiTest.groups, which builds an HttpApiClient that dispatches through an in-memory HttpClient wired straight to HttpApiBuilder.layer(api). Optional baseUrl override landed in commit 33d26b4. Watchlist in docs/EFFECT_CHANGELOG.md already flags this as the canonical test harness. Add a changelog bullet under the introducing beta (b63), and consider an HttpApi server story that exercises HttpApiTest.groups for endpoint testing without binding a port.
close_reason: Added 'Testing HttpApi handlers without a server: HttpApiTest' reference section to docs/11-testing.md, including a representative code snippet and a note that the storybook lacks a platform layer (no @effect/platform-bun yet). Watchlist entry rewritten to reflect this. Runnable example deferred until an HttpApi server story is added — that's a separate scope (would require pulling in @effect/platform-bun).

title: Bump effect deps to beta.66 + apply audit findings
description: Now that beta.66 is published, bump effect ^4.0.0-beta.57 → ^4.0.0-beta.66 and @effect/vitest ^4.0.0-beta.58 → ^4.0.0-beta.66 in package.json, run bun install, run vitest + bun:test + typecheck to verify nothing breaks, then apply the storybook audit findings unblocked by the bump: (1) drop the 'Schema.make not yet on branded schemas' caveat in stories/02 and docs/02 — beta.44 brought the instance .make back; (2) consider Config.literals(['dev','prod']) shorthand in stories/06 (b60). Skip findings that don't apply.
close_reason: bumped effect + @effect/vitest 4.0.0-beta.57/58 → beta.66; gates green (typecheck no errors, vitest 6/6, bun:test 1/1); applied audit findings — story 02 + doc 02 now use .make() instead of Schema.decodeUnknownSync(Brand); CHANGES.md and EFFECT_CHANGELOG.md updated to retire the resolved workaround items; codebase target line bumped to beta.66

title: Extend EFFECT_CHANGELOG.md to beta.66
description: docs/EFFECT_CHANGELOG.md currently stops at 4.0.0-beta.58. New betas published since commit 37b7971: beta.59, .60, .62, .63, .64, .65, .66 (beta.61 was skipped on npm). Pull each release's notes from the effect-smol CHANGELOG, distill to storybook-relevant items (Schema/Effect/Layer/Context/Config/unstable/http/tagged errors/testing), append new sections, update the header line and quick-map table if any chapter is affected, and refresh the watchlist.
close_reason: appended beta.59–beta.66 sections; updated chapter quick-map (01/02/04/05/06/11) + watchlist (Yieldable removal, Schema.make rename, Config.literals, DurationFromString, HttpApiTest); header bumped to beta.66

Update CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md to permit @effect/vitest for Effect tests while keeping bun:test guidance for non-Effect code, install deps, add vitest config, rewrite stories/11-testing.test.ts, and refresh docs/11-testing.md to flip the recommendation.

title: Document Missionary ↔ Effect-TS primitive comparison
description: Add a reference doc under docs/ that maps each Missionary (Clojure, leonoel/missionary b.47) primitive to its Effect-TS (effect@4.0.0-beta.57) counterpart. Includes Task/Flow/Signal mental model, the missing R parameter on the Missionary side, and a porting cheat-sheet in both directions. Source-verified against upstream missionary/master.

title: Note formatJson circular-ref fix and stringifyCircular dedup in changelog
description: Fix: formatJson now handles circular references correctly. Cleanup: removed a duplicated stringifyCircular utility. Add a changelog bullet under the affected beta.
close_reason: Already documented in docs/EFFECT_CHANGELOG.md beta.60 line 55: 'Inspectable.stringifyCircular removed; Formatter.formatJson preserves shared (non-circular) object references and only elides true cycles.' No further action.

title: Note fiber lifetime metric start-hook lifecycle fix in changelog
description: Fix: fiber lifetime metric start hook was firing at the wrong lifecycle point and has been moved. Add a changelog bullet. Affects observability accuracy for users on fiber lifetime histograms.
close_reason: Already documented in docs/EFFECT_CHANGELOG.md beta.61 line 50: 'Fiber-runtime start metrics recorded at construction (yielded fibers no longer double-counted).' Matches the user's description of moving the start hook to the correct lifecycle point. No further action.

title: Note OpenTelemetry logger severity mapping fix in changelog
description: Bug fix: OTel logger integration mapped severity incorrectly (likely Effect log levels → OTel SeverityNumber). Add a changelog bullet under the affected beta.
close_reason: Added bullet to docs/EFFECT_CHANGELOG.md beta.61 noting @effect/opentelemetry OtelLogger.make now emits spec SeverityNumber (1-24) instead of LogLevel ordinal. Includes the helper export logLevelToSeverityNumber.

title: Note OpenAI config fields no longer spread into request body
description: Bug fix: AI provider integration was spreading OpenAI config fields into the request body. Add a changelog bullet under the affected beta. We don't have an AI provider story to update.
close_reason: Out of scope for this storybook. The curated changelog explicitly skips AI: 'Skipped: AI / LanguageModel / EmbeddingModel, MCP, Workflow, Cluster, RPC internals, CLI prompt UX, Atom reactivity.' The fix is in @effect/ai-openai (commit 5be0aaa, beta.63), which the storybook doesn't use. Anyone affected reads the upstream packages/ai-openai CHANGELOG.

title: Note HttpApiBuilder empty-body decoding fix in changelog
description: Bug fix in upstream Effect: HttpApiBuilder mishandled empty bodies during decode. Add a changelog bullet under the affected beta. No story change expected unless story 07 grows an HttpApi server example.
close_reason: Already documented in docs/EFFECT_CHANGELOG.md beta.61 line 49: 'HttpApiBuilder correctly decodes empty bodies.' No further action.

title: Document Rpc.custom in changelog
description: Adds Rpc.custom for defining custom RPC handlers with full control over the request/response lifecycle. No RPC story exists yet, so just add a changelog bullet under the introducing beta. File a follow-up if/when an RPC story is added.
close_reason: Already documented in docs/EFFECT_CHANGELOG.md beta.60 line 59: '(RPC) Rpc.custom.' Tagged (RPC) since the storybook intro skips RPC internals — sufficient mention for the curated audience. No further action.

title: Note Kubernetes condition.lastTransitionTime allows null in changelog
description: Platform fix: K8s condition lastTransitionTime now accepts null to handle optional fields in real K8s manifests. Add a changelog bullet under the affected beta. Low priority — only relevant if we add a K8s/platform-k8s story.
close_reason: Already documented in docs/EFFECT_CHANGELOG.md beta.62 line 45: 'Narrow K8s schema relaxation (lastTransitionTime nullable). No storybook impact.' No further action.

value: effect@4.0.0-beta.57 (effect-smol) differs from the 4.0.0-beta.31 baseline documented in tbiz_ts/EFFECT_V4_MIGRATION.md: (1) ServiceMap module renamed to Context — use Context.Service<Self, Shape>()('id'). (2) Effect.catchAll removed — use Effect.match / Effect.matchEffect / Effect.catchTag instead. (3) Schema.withDecodingDefault takes an Effect<Encoded>, not a thunk — pass Effect.succeed(value). (4) ConfigProvider.fromUnknown does not auto-split env-style underscores — provide nested objects ({DB: {HOST: ...}}) or use ConfigProvider.fromEnv. (5) HttpClient.make validates URLs up-front — even mock clients need absolute URLs. (6) HttpClientRequest.bodyJson returns Effect<Request>, so yield it before passing to execute (or use bodyJsonUnsafe). (7) Schedule.intersect renamed to Schedule.both.

effect-smol source clone is at ~/.local/share/effect-solutions/effect — authoritative reference for Effect API shapes, idioms, and test patterns. Consult before writing Effect code, especially: packages/effect/test/*.test.ts for idiomatic usage, packages/effect/src/*.ts for source, packages/effect/CHANGELOG.md for full version notes. The clone is kept fresh via 'cd ~/.local/share/effect-solutions/effect && git pull'. Prefer reading from this clone over guessing or relying on node_modules .d.ts excerpts. CLAUDE.md says the same; this memory makes it stick across sessions.

effectts_hello beads now syncs via dolt to refs/dolt/data on reedho/effectts_hello (remote 'origin' = git+ssh://git@github.com/reedho/effectts_hello.git). DOLT PUSH WORKS warm. GOTCHA (solved 2026-06-20): bd 1.0.4 embedded-dolt DEADLOCKS on the FIRST push to an EMPTY refs/dolt/data (hangs at 'Pushing to Dolt remote...', futex holding noms/LOCK + git-remote-cache/init.lock, no network). WORKAROUND that fixed it: (1) seed the cold remote by pushing any valid dolt-data commit via plain git/OpenSSH, borrowed from a warm beads repo cache: git --git-dir=/home/ridho/github/mynixes2026/.beads/embeddeddolt/nix/.dolt/git-remote-cache/<hash>/repo.git push git@github.com:reedho/effectts_hello.git <commit>:refs/dolt/data ; (2) then 'bd dolt push --force' from the real DB overwrites refs/dolt/data with correct data (cache-init now fetches the non-empty ref instead of deadlocking). After seeding, normal 'bd dolt push' works. Same root cause/fix as mynixes2026-beads-sync-model.

effectts_hello toolchain: bun (v1.3.14) is installed via the single-binary installer at ~/.bun/bin/bun and is now ON the login-shell PATH (added by the user 2026-06-20) — no need to export BUN_INSTALL/PATH anymore; just run bun/bun run directly. typescript 5.x is a peerDependency (no node_modules/.bin/tsc symlink); 'bun run typecheck' works because bun adds the local tsc. GOTCHA: a partial 'bun install' can omit bun-types (transitive dep of @types/bun) — symptom is tsc errors 'Cannot find module bun:test' / 'Cannot find name Bun'; fix with 'bun install --force'. Gates: bun run typecheck (0 errors expected; @effect/language-service emits advisory TS35/36/42/44/70 warnings on the storybook anti-pattern examples — those are intentional, not failures), bun run test (vitest 6/6), bun run test:bun (1/1).
